## PR #2659: Melee decomp: SysDolphin

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: src/sysdolphin/baselib;axdriver;cobj;particle;sislib

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2659/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2643: Match grInishie2_801FD4F0

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: stage;grInishie2;ground

A one-line stage decomp matching PR adjusted the stack padding in src/melee/gr/grinishie2.c for grInishie2_801FD4F0 from PAD_STACK(16) to PAD_STACK(12). The PR body says the prior pad placed a local Vec3 at 0x24(r1), while the adjusted pad moves it to the expected 0x20(r1), producing a 100% objdiff match. The bot report confirms one new match for main/melee/gr/grinishie2, grInishie2_801FD4F0, from 99.91% to 100.00%.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2643/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2542: Add MetroTRK exception vector assembly

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: MetroTRK;.init;config/GALE01;Nix build;dtk/binutils

Added a dedicated assembly split for the MetroTRK exception vector table in .init, mapping 0x80003298-0x800051CC to src/MetroTRK/__exception.s. The PR represented this region in assembly rather than C because it is layout-sensitive and mixes embedded bytes, fixed-size gaps, labels, and instructions in one contiguous block. It also updated the GALE01 split file and taught the Nix configure step to pass devkitPPC binutils so dtk can assemble the new source without network downloads.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2542/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2493: Match hsd_80394314

Status: agent_completed
Type: closed-invalid-mismatched-symbol
Systems: src/sysdolphin/baselib;particle

Draft PR claimed to match `hsd_80394314` in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/particle.c`, but the only diff changed the storage declaration for `hsd_804CF810` in `particle.static.h` by removing `static`. The author closed it after recognizing a title/body-vs-diff symbol mismatch; decomp-dev's no-progress/no-changes verdict for the labeled symbol was correct. The useful takeaway is process-oriented: automated PR generation needs a pre-open soundness gate to verify that the labeled target symbol, diff, and objdiff progress actually agree before consuming reviewer time.

Automated security-fix PR against an external Dolphin charPipeline header. It changed only the declarations of Strcat and Strcpy in dolphinString.h to add a u32 dst_size parameter. The slice contains no review comments, no implementation or callsite updates, and no merge evidence.

## PR #2467: Fix various literals and frame sizes

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching cleanup
Systems: fighter;game-mode;stage;interface;item;library;menu;melee-core;trophy;video;sysdolphin-baselib

Bulk matching cleanup from two scripts: one corrected literals, and one adjusted stack frame sizes by adding, removing, or changing padding. The diff spans 84 source files across fighter, game-mode, stage, interface, item, library, menu, trophy, video, and sysdolphin code. Evidence shows many PAD_STACK or explicit u8 padding changes plus representative literal/control-flow edits. Bot report shows +212 matched-code bytes, +16 matched-data bytes, 2 new matches, 192 unmatched-item improvements, and 1 unmatched .sdata2 regression in gmresult. ## PR #2417: Fix build from macOS

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_fix
Systems: tools/project.py;build wrapper selection;macOS build

PR #2417 is a small tooling fix for macOS builds. It changes tools/project.py so use_wibo only returns true on Linux, removing darwin from the automatic wibo path. The PR body says macOS builds were trying to execute build/tools/wibo instead of using wine, causing '/bin/sh: build/tools/wibo: cannot execute binary file'; the after log shows the progress command completing.

## PR #2217: Match ~100 functions

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: effect;fighter;fighter-common;kirby;popo;crazy-hand;game-mode;stage;item;library;menu;vi;baselib

Large AI-assisted decomp matching PR touching 92 files across fighter, item, stage, menu/game-mode, library, VI, and baselib areas. The PR body says the author used an AI harness, filtered out matches with regressions, avoided obvious cheats such as inline assembly, and claimed about +0.2% global match-rate improvement. Bot report evidence in the loaded text corpus reports +0.23% matched code, 103 new matches, 7 unmatched-item improvements, and 7 unmatched-item regressions. Review feedback focused on wrong section placement for VI sbss data, raw Fighter pointer arithmetic that should use fields or at least M2C_FIELD, avoiding NOT_IMPLEMENTED stub bodies as matches, missing prototypes, and C99 declaration-rule issues.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2217/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Type: decomp-matching
Systems: game-mode;gm;gm_1832;gmallstar;gmregtyfall;gmtitle;fighter;player;lb;baselib;sobjlib

Miscellaneous game-mode decomp work centered on gm_1832, All-Star, Trophy Fall, and shared HSD_SObj/JObj typing. The PR fully matched gm_1832 functions fn_801859C8 and fn_80185D64, made gmTitle_801A1E20_OnEnter fully match, substantially implemented gm_801B60A4_OnLoad, and improved gmregtyfall matching by replacing verbose null-safe child traversal and inline-expanded JObj setter code with matching helper wrappers/static-header definitions. It also cleaned up baselib SObj naming from HSD_SObj_803A477C_t to HSD_SObj and propagated more accurate HSD_JObj return types through ftLib_800865F0 and Player_80036EA0.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2157/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2156: Decompile Big Blue stage functions (grbigblue, grbigblueroute)

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: stage;melee-core;ground;collision;item;baselib

Large Big Blue stage decompilation PR covering grbigblue.c and grbigblueroute.c, with 10 files changed and nearly 3k added lines. It replaced many placeholder `/// #symbol` entries with C implementations, added Big Blue-specific static declarations/types, exposed needed prototypes in ground/mp/grfzerocar headers, and improved names for local variables and callbacks. The PR included several partial or near-matching functions, with explicit TODO comments documenting match percentages and remaining assembly issues such as register allocation, stack offsets, and addressing mode differences. Human discussion was minimal; the only excerpted comment noted that partial matches represented substantial progress even if the bot did not count them fully.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2156/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2154: [WIP] mnsnap decompile

Status: agent_completed
Type: decompilation
Systems: melee/mn;mnsnap;GALE01 symbols;baselib;lbcard;lbsnap;SIS text;HSD JObj/DObj/MObj/TObj

Large decompilation PR for the snapshot/gallery memory-card menu module `mnsnap`, adding roughly 2.5k lines to `src/melee/mn/mnsnap.c`, updating its header, and replacing a coarse `mnSnap_803F0168` data symbol with more precise local string symbols in `config/GALE01/symbols.txt`. The PR author noted that `fn_802545C4` and `mnSnap_80257F24` remained massive and were only as good as practical for now, while the rest was intended for review. Review focused less on broad behavior and more on decomp hygiene: repeated HSD texture image-descriptor assertion chains likely indicate a shared inline from `tobj.h`, match-percentage comments are noise because objdiff already reports them, and meaningful `@todo` notes should use doc-comment syntax so they appear in generated TODO pages.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2154/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2153: Decompile item functions with Codex

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: item;common-items;character-items;pokemon-items;stage-items

Decompiled a batch of relatively simple item functions, using Codex as an agent-assisted decompilation experiment. The PR replaced many placeholder `/// #...` stubs with matching C in item source files, especially `itrshell.c`, and added or refined item-var structs in `itCommonItems.h`, `itCharItems.h`, and `types.h` so the implementations could access the correct `xDD4_itemVar` union members. The author noted that Codex was workable when given good Skills/tooling, but needed reminders about selecting the right union member and still struggled with larger functions. Some RShell matches required unusual `goto` control flow that the author considered unlikely but apparently necessary to preserve the match.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2153/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2147: Toy matches

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: melee-core;toy/trophy;language;baselib-gobj;baselib-jobj;baselib-lobj

Large decompilation pass on src/melee/ty/toy, described by the author as “More vacuuming work.” The PR replaced many /// stubs in toy.c with C implementations, added numerous local toy/trophy/camera/light/display structs and externs needed to express data access, and adjusted toy.h declarations. The match report recorded +2680 matched bytes, 10 new full matches in main/melee/ty/toy, and 18 improved unmatched items, including several 90–99% toy functions and .sdata/.sdata2 progress. Review focused on keeping generated decomp output maintainable: remove useless sandbox comments, keep type definitions at the top of the file, prefer structured loops and continue over goto next_iter, and consider inline helpers for label-shaped boolean checks unless doing so hurts matching.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2147/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Type: decomp-matching
Systems: stage;ground;Venom;Corneria helpers;baselib JObj/GObj;mpLib;audio;lighting

## PR #2143: Miscellaneous matches

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;game-mode;item;stage;toy;interface

Large automated "vacuum system" PR that converted many small unmatched functions across fighter, game-mode, stage, item, interface, and toy code into C matches while updating headers, structs, and item/stage union fields. The PR was valuable both for the matches and for exposing recurring agent/m2c failure modes: wrong union enum variants, overuse of void*/raw offsets, local ad-hoc structs that belong in shared headers, and accepting fake matches that should be reverted until types are fixed. Review feedback led to concrete guardrails and static quality checks for future autonomous decomp work.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2143/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2139: result screen (gmresult) matches

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: game-mode;gmresult;result-screen;stats-ui

Matched a large portion of the result-screen game-mode translation unit by replacing multiple gmresult.c asm stubs with C, adding local result-stat table structs, and adjusting gmresult headers/static declarations. The work centered on stats-list filtering/rendering, text creation and formatting for result stats, KO/time display routines, and related helper functions. The PR also exposed a regression/prototype discussion around fn_80174A60 returning u8: the author claimed it matched that function but caused caller code to emit extra clrlwi masking, while reviewers pushed back that the explanation was likely overconfident and that inline implementation issues, stack misalignments, and float/double discrepancies were more plausible areas to revisit. The merge was accepted despite the regression because breaking a same-TU function in an unlinked TU was considered acceptable.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2139/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2138: Kirby Copy Abilities matches

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;ftKirby;Kirby copy abilities;item;game-mode;build-config

Large Kirby copy-ability decomp/matching PR that split the former monolithic ftKb_Init.c Kirby range into multiple ftKb_SpecialN* translation units and added thousands of lines of partial/matching C for Kirby neutral-special/copy-ability behavior. It updated GALE01 splits and configure.py to build the new objects, added/refined Kirby types and item headers, and made small call-site/type fixes in CaptureKirby, items, and result-player code. Review centered on accepting incremental LLM-assisted partial matches while cleaning obvious slop: prefer GET_FIGHTER over raw gobj->user_data, use motion-state and FighterKind enums instead of raw integers, avoid unnecessary casts by fixing variable/field types, and rename generic var_* loop temporaries to i/j/result. The final discussion explicitly accepted a small match-percent regression from GET_FIGHTER cleanup as worthwhile because matched locked-in functions still diffed cleanly.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2138/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2134: Wip/logf decompilation

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: MSL;MSL/math;config/GALE01

Decompiled and substantially improved matching for MSL math logf by rewriting the function around explicit IEEE-754 bit manipulation and moving math lookup tables out of src/MSL/math.c into a new src/MSL/math_data.c compilation unit. The PR accepted a known .rodata regression because separating the tables prevented mwcc from hoisting shared array base addresses, raising logf from about 59.82% to 99.80% matched with all 91 target instructions identical except for a stack-frame-size mismatch.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2134/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2102: Match a bunch of "duplicate" functions with Claude

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;item;stage;game-mode;library;menu;melee-core

The work spanned 131 files across fighter, item, stage, game-mode, library, and menu code, replacing many `/// #symbol` stubs with C implementations and adding the headers/prototypes/includes needed for those matches. The PR is explicitly described as a partial batch of remaining duplicate functions, with the author emphasizing that duplicate matches are not safe cut-and-paste because equivalent assembly can involve different local types. Review focused on correcting AI-generated type/float/include mistakes: using item-specific `itemVar` structs instead of borrowing `linkhookshot`, preserving floating-point literal forms for `Fighter_ChangeMotionState`, using the right baselib include path, and fixing mistaken generator/app-srt field interpretation.

## PR #2076: Match itleadead.c

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: item;melee-core;fighter;stage-collision

Matched the Leadead item source file, replacing many placeholder stubs in src/melee/it/items/itleadead.c with C implementations and adding the item-specific structs/prototypes needed to express them. The PR also corrected related typing/signature issues, including adding the leadead member to Item_ItemVars, defining itLeadead_ItemVars and itLeadeadAttributes, tightening an itlinkarrow prototype, and reducing mpLib_80056C54 to the arguments actually used. The author explicitly framed this as a tooling-assisted decomp experiment: Claude Code, custom Claude Skills, m2c enhancements for union fields/void pointer field types/struct copies, complexity sorting, and an automated checkdiff.py iteration loop produced most of the matches. decomp.dev reported 51 new matches and +8516 matched bytes.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2076/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Expanded Green Greens stage decompilation by replacing many placeholder grGreens functions with C implementations, adding stage callback tables, stage parameter globals, block/wind helper logic, and stronger typed prototypes. The PR also introduced Green Greens-specific Ground vars and block vars in gr/types.h, renamed key symbols for the Green Greens init function and data globals, and adjusted adjacent APIs in ground, grmaterial, and ftdevice so the new stage code could call them with real types. Human review evidence is absent; the only comment was a decomp.dev bot report saying the GALE01 report was not found.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2038/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2037: lb: Match library functions

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: library;lb;audio;background-flash;movie-thp;refract;snapshot

Matched or partially implemented several melee/lb library functions across lbAudioAx, lbMthp, lbRefract, lbBgFlash, and lbSnap. The PR replaced multiple NonMatching stubs with C implementations, refined headers with concrete signatures, added missing GX/baselib includes, and introduced local/static struct definitions needed for matching. Review feedback emphasized that matching should not come at the cost of raw pointer/offset accesses: offsets should be represented through real datatypes when possible, or at least through M2C_FIELD for grepability and future decomp reuse.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2037/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2011: Add dependency graph analysis tool

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: tooling;build-analysis;configure.py object metadata;GALE01 report metadata

Added `tools/dep_graph.py`, a standalone Python tool that builds object-file symbol dependency and reverse-dependency graphs from `configure.py`, built `.o` files, `nm` output, and optional `build/GALE01/report.json` metadata. Its main decompilation use is finding leaf `NonMatching` files that can potentially be converted to `Matching` without leaving other `NonMatching` files depending on them, plus identifying high-impact files whose conversion would unlock other work.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2011/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: external-sdk;dolphin;thp;video-decoding

## PR #1997: Wip

Status: agent_completed
Type: unmerged_wip_decompilation
Systems: fighter;ftCommon;ft_0C88;walk_turn_motion;cloaking_device;barrel_item;downspot;item;game-mode;player

Closed, unmerged WIP PR that appears to have been opened/closed accidentally: the only human comment says, "OOPS i meant to merge yalls into mine lol". The diff was nevertheless substantial: it replaced many stub markers in src/melee/ft/ft_0C88.c with partial/common-fighter implementations around cloaking-device state, DownSpot, Barrel item carry, walk, and turn handling, and it added the supporting motion-variable union members, inline helpers, function prototypes, and more specific types needed to compile those attempts. Because the PR was not reviewed or merged and includes comments such as "rough, look at later" and large commented-out functions, treat it as evidence of exploratory matching work rather than accepted project practice.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1997/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1955: match some ftKb_SpecialN functions

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;Kirby;ftKb_SpecialN;fighter-parts

Matched a batch of Kirby copy-ability hat setup functions in ftKb_Init.c, mostly by recognizing duplicate ftKb_SpecialN routines that allocate Kirby hat part data, load a hat joint, mark x2225_b2, attach the JObj through ftParts helpers, and initialize joint metadata. The PR also replaced several raw hat-table indices with FTKIND_* enum constants, clarifying that ft_80459B88.hats is indexed by fighter kind. The author noted uncertainty around ftKb_SpecialN_800EFA40 and the layout of ft_80459B88_t, initially seeing a possible negative array access, then resolving the match by using the separate x0 Kirby_Unk field rather than changing the struct to make hats start at offset 0.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1955/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1954: Implement nearly all remaining "proxy" functions

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;kirby;popo;ftcommon;stage;menu;toy;tooling

Large decomp-matching pass that replaced many `/// #` placeholders for simple proxy/trampoline functions with C bodies that only call one other function. The work was found with a new `table-typer dups` command, which grouped duplicated asm bodies and identified unmatched functions sharing the same opcode sequence as known proxy functions. The decomp.dev bot reported 114 new matches and +3648 matched bytes. Most changes landed in Kirby copy-ability motion callbacks, with smaller proxy implementations in ftCommon, Popo, stage files, menu, and toy code. The author deliberately left 8 proxy functions unimplemented because they lacked placeholders; follow-up comment clarified those TUs likely had not been scaffolded yet.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1954/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1913: Remove DEBUG/NDEBUG flags from build script

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_fix
Systems: repo-root;configure.py;build-script;objdiff-debug-info

Removed automatic DEBUG/NDEBUG preprocessor definitions from configure.py's debug flag handling. The --debug option now only enables Metrowerks symbol output with "-sym on" and no longer adds "-DDEBUG=1"; non-debug builds no longer add "-sym off" or "-DNDEBUG=1" from this block. The PR body says these defines were only used by extern/dolphin/ and caused build failures when using --debug to get line number information in objdiff.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1913/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1899: Finish controller match with cursed fake match

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: sysdolphin;baselib;controller;config;symbols

Finished matching sysdolphin/baselib/controller.c by getting HSD_PadADConvertCheck1 from 99.99% to 100%, then flipped controller.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The fix used a deliberately awkward/cursed source rewrite: small static inline vector helpers, an apparently redundant r = sq(x) assignment before recomputing r, expansion of atan2f_check behavior with fabs(x) == 0.0f handling, and an early return instead of a nested !(r < adc_th) block. Symbols were also tightened so several controller helper functions and sdata2 constants became local labels/symbols rather than exported HSD_Controller_* globals.

## PR #1879: Restore `ftAction_80071028`

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter

Restored the decompiled implementation of fighter script handler `ftAction_80071028`, replacing a fully commented-out draft with matching C. The function reads several consecutive command event payloads, updates the command pointer between fixed 4-byte script records, converts packed offsets/ranges by multiplying by `1 / 256.0f`, and spawns fighter GFX through `ftCo_8009F834` unless the fighter is invisible, in which case it skips the GFX spawn via `ftAction_800711DC`. The supporting inline macro `gmScriptEventUpdatePtr` was changed to advance the event pointer by 4 bytes instead of `sizeof(type)`, which appears central to matching this command-stream layout.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1879/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1878: Merge `CommandInfo` structs

Status: agent_completed
Type: shared struct/type unification refactor
Systems: fighter;item;library;game-mode;stage

Merged the fighter/game-mode command-script state and library CommandInfo representations into one shared `CommandInfo` type under `lb/types.h`, then retargeted fighter and item command execution code to use it. The PR is primarily a cross-system type/header refactor: `FtCmdState` was removed from `gm/types.h`, fighter `ftCmdScript` storage became `CommandInfo`, command callbacks now take `CommandInfo*`, and command pointer/timer fields were renamed to typed names such as `u`, `timer`, `frame_count`, and `loop_count`. Evidence is limited by an empty PR body and no human review comments; the only comment is a decomp.dev report showing multiple unmatched-function regressions after the merge.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1878/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1845: Bump objdiff to v3.0.0 and wibo to 0.7.0

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_dependency_bump
Systems: .nix;repo-root;configure.py;objdiff;wibo;devkitppc;m2c

Updated repository tooling around decomp comparison and Windows-exe execution: objdiff was bumped from v2.7.1 to v3.0.0, wibo from 0.6.16 to 0.7.0, nixpkgs was advanced, and supporting Nix packaging fixes were added. The PR mattered because objdiff 3 changed reported fuzzy/matched/linked percentages and how hard-coded labels are handled, initially making the diff bot appear to show broken matches. Review discussion concluded the apparent linked-code regression was expected/benign after updating the branch, with remaining reported issues being global jump table labels in GXPerf rather than real function breakage.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1845/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1838: allow for specific ifdef preservation in ctx

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: tooling;effect;fighter;stage;item;menu;toy;melee-core

Reworked tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py so the pcpp path uses a custom Preprocessor subclass instead of invoking pcpp with a static command-line flag list. The custom preprocessor preserves selected conditional blocks keyed on M2C while still defining M2CTX for context generation, carrying over the original include paths, defines, undefines, compression/comment behavior, and MWERKS compatibility assumptions. Header guards and inline/type helper conditionals across effect, fighter, ground, item, menu, and toy headers were switched from #ifdef M2CTX to #ifdef M2C so those decomp.me/m2c-specific alternatives can survive into ctx output rather than being eagerly resolved during context preprocessing.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1838/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: melee/if/iftime;melee/if/ifall;melee/gm;config/GALE01;melee/mn/types

Matched and named most of the interface timer unit, adding a new C implementation for src/melee/if/iftime.c and replacing address-style ifTime/fn symbols with semantic names for timer creation, update, visibility, countdown, reset, and teardown. The PR also propagated those names into headers and game-mode call sites, adjusted GALE01 split/symbol sizes for iftime data/BSS, and renamed a StartMeleeRules bitfield to timer_shows_hours to support the timer formatting logic. The decomp.dev bot reported 8 new fully matched iftime functions and partial matches for SetTime, CreateTimers, and UpdateTimers.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1788/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1786: Include main header first

Status: agent_completed
Type: formatting_config
Systems: repo-root;build-style

Updated the repository .clang-format include-category priorities so clang-format places a C file's corresponding main header first, e.g. fighter.c including fighter.h before all other includes. The rationale was to make each main header self-sufficient: if the corresponding .h is included first in its .c file, missing standalone dependencies are exposed instead of being hidden by earlier transitive includes.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1786/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1784: Add tools/table-typer and infer more types

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling-assisted type inference
Systems: tooling;item;repo-root

Added a new Go utility, tools/table-typer, to infer concrete function signatures from table declarations and asm data, currently hard-coded for ItemStateTable. The PR also applied the tool's output across many item files, replacing UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS or void(void) placeholders with bool/void Item_GObj* callback signatures. This improved header/source type precision for item state-table callbacks without changing GALE01 output.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1784/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1773: Match lb_80011AC4 with new LightList type

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: library;scene;ground;fighter;game-mode;menu;melee-core

Introduced a typed SceneDesc light-list representation and used it to fully match lb_80011AC4. The PR replaces ad hoc HSD_LightDesc**/UNK_T/fake node structs with LightList and LightList** across library, scene, fighter, menu, title, and ground code. lb_80011AC4 now constructs an HSD_LObj linked list from a NULL-terminated LightList** array, loads each HSD_LightDesc, attaches the first HSD_LightAnim when present, and links the resulting HSD_LObj nodes. Partially decompiled the main menu OnEnter routine mn_8022DDA8_OnEnter in src/melee/mn/mn_2295.c, raising its reported match from 0.00% to 99.20%. The PR replaced the function stub with C that initializes menu state, conditionally loads MnMaAll archive symbols, loads SIS menu/toy data, creates lights/fog/camera/background/panel objects, dispatches special event/100-man paths, and starts audio/state setup. It also renamed many anonymous main-menu asset globals to archive-derived MenMain* names, split a large .data blob into individual string labels in symbols.txt, added mnevent.h declarations, and corrected several prototypes needed by the new C.

## PR #1720: Work around `ninja diff` return code

Type: tooling_ci_fix
Systems: .github;tools;config;GALE01 symbols

## PR #1717: gmtou matches

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: game-mode;tournament-mode;gmtou;gm_17C0;baselib;lbarchive;lbaudio

Matched several Tournament mode gmtou functions and tightened related game-mode declarations. The PR replaced multiple gmtou stubs with C implementations for on-enter/on-leave archive setup and cleanup, animation timer callbacks, and JObj animation-frame selection logic. It also introduced Tournament-specific data structures such as TmData, TmBoxArrays, and TmAnimTimers, converted gm_804771C4 from an untyped array placeholder to TmData, and improved prototypes in gmtou.h and gm_17C0.h from UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS/UNK_T to concrete HSD_GObj, HSD_JObj, void, char*, and TmData* types. Review feedback highlighted an important header hygiene issue: .static.h headers containing static/data definitions should only be included from .c files, not public headers, because including them elsewhere can perturb data/bss sections.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1706/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: fighter;ftSeak;effect;build-config

## PR #1690: Add translation unit support for decomp.py

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: tools;m2c integration;decomp.py

Updated tools/decomp.py so its single positional m2c input can be either a function name or a translation-unit path such as melee/it/items/itheiho. The script now attempts to resolve the input as a function via find_obj/has_function; if no object containing that function is found, it treats the input as a translation unit, builds the corresponding ASM path, and invokes m2c without --function. --write remains function-only and now errors for translation-unit input.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1690/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1638: Match gobjproc

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: sysdolphin;baselib;gobjproc;configure.py

Matched sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc.c by implementing HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8, the GObjProc insertion routine, and marking the object Matching in configure.py. The implementation replaces NOT_IMPLEMENTED and removed dont_inline pragmas with C control flow that scans GObj/proc lists by p_link and s_link, updates global proc-list anchors, links the new proc into next/prev/child chains, and preserves current-proc globals. The author noted the control flow is ugly but matches; decomp.dev reported one new function match, HSD_GObjProc_8038FAA8, +365 bytes, taking main/sysdolphin/baselib/gobjproc from 1.09% to 100.00%.

## PR #1603: Port over THP from Pikmin 2

Status: agent_completed
Type: external-sdk-port-and-decomp-matching
Systems: external-sdk;dolphin-thp;config;symbols

Added a large THP decoder implementation by porting THP code/structure knowledge from Pikmin 2 into Melee's Dolphin external SDK area. The PR introduced extern/dolphin/src/dolphin/thp/THPDec.c and extern/dolphin/include/dolphin/thp/thp.h, then renamed several previously anonymous THPDec_* symbols in config/GALE01/symbols.txt to confident THP names. The PR body explains the central matching insight: other decompiled games use a later THP version with a global THPFileInfo pointer instead of passing THPFileInfo into each private function, and adapting to that global-pointer style was enough to match or near-match several functions.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1603/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1578: Match OS, OSReset

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: external-sdk;dolphin-os;os-init;os-reset;threading;memory-protection;repo-root

Matched Dolphin OS.c and OSReset.c by completing missing SDK initialization/reset behavior, adding required internal prototypes and an OSThread API declaration, and flipping both objects from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py. The OSInit changes add startup timestamp capture, BI2/debug flag handling, alarm and memory-protection init calls, HID2 masking, arena clearing, and more accurate MetroTRK/debug handling. The OSReset changes add hot reset support, active-thread cancellation, reset-type-specific reboot/reset behavior, cache/LC and memory-clear handling, pad recalibration preservation, and an adjusted OSGetResetCode implementation.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1578/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1447: Match itdkinoko

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: item;items/itdkinoko;asm;configure.py;obj_files.mk

Matched the ditto/poison mushroom item module `itdkinoko` by replacing the 305-line assembly file with a 148-line C implementation, expanding the header prototypes, and moving the object from asm/nonmatching to src/matching in the build configuration. ## PR #1446: Update `dtk-template`

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling-template-update
Systems: .github/workflows;.nix;configure.py;tools/project.py;tools/download_tool.py;tools/decompctx.py;repo-root

Updated the repository's dtk-template-derived tooling and CI integration around decomp-toolkit 0.9.6, objdiff-cli report generation, and richer progress/report metadata. The main work refactored tools/project.py to resolve object/library options centrally, add ProgressCategory support, generate report.json as a Ninja build target, support objdiff-cli as a downloaded/built tool, and adjust configure.py to use the new template conventions. Review discussion focused on the transitional CI behavior caused by checking out commits before master had the new report.json Ninja rule.

Status: agent_completed

Systems: MSL;runtime library;asm;configure.py;obj_files.mk

## PR #1438: Foxblaster demystification and Lgun decomp

Status: agent_completed
Type: item decompilation and documentation/demystification
Systems: item;FoxBlaster;FalcoBlaster;Kirby blaster copy ability items;L-Gun;JObj item model transforms;item collision

PR 1438 documented and partially demystified Fox/Falco/Kirby blaster item behavior while adding a full C implementation for the L-Gun item. The FoxBlasterVars struct kept offset-based unknown fields but gained semantic names for confirmed roles such as angle, gfx_spawn_var, set_sfx_var2, and owner, with extensive comments tying cmd_vars/subaction flags to visibility, shot spawning, put-away behavior, JObj transforms, and effect/SFX triggers. itfoxblaster.c was cleaned up with item_gobj parameter names, Doxygen-style briefs, typed prototypes for spawn functions, and a small apparent SFX addition for Falco blaster put-away. itlgun.c went from effectively empty to 240 lines of item state-table callbacks, spawn/init, attribute extraction, ground/air transitions, timers, animation state changes, collision responses, and pickup/reference handling. Header updates added ItLGunAttr, ItLGunVars, the lgun item-var union member, and more specific item-spawn signatures.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1438/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1407: Run `make format`

Type: formatting_cleanup
Systems: runtime;dolphin;fighter;stage;ground;item;menu;library;baselib;melee-core

The changes are almost entirely mechanical clang-format output: pointer-star placement, spacing around operators and casts, include ordering, line wrapping, brace placement, comment alignment, and blank-line normalization. ## PR #1406: m2ctx: Pass `--quiet` to error output

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_error_output
Systems: tools/m2ctx

Updated the m2ctx tooling so that preprocessing failures respect the `--quiet` flag: the failing command is still printed to stderr, but the full preprocessor output/context dump is suppressed when quiet mode is enabled. The patch threaded a typed `quiet: bool` parameter through `try_import`, `pcpp_import`, and `mwcc_import`, and adjusted error printing to avoid messy trailing-newline concatenation.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1406/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1405: Rewrite diff workflow to make it compare properly

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: .github/workflows/build.yml;tools/diff_changes.py;GitHub Actions;objdiff-cli

Reworked the GitHub Actions diff-check workflow so objdiff compares the intended expected and actual reports, then moved the broken-function detection logic out of inline jq and into a new Python helper, tools/diff_changes.py. The workflow now records the original HEAD before checking out the comparison target, rebuilds the expected report there, checks back out HEAD, generates an objdiff changes report, and runs the Python script to fail CI when any previously 100% fuzzy-matched function regresses.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1405/postmortem/postmortem.json`

PR #1364 completed the JObj decompilation match by deleting the remaining sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.s assembly file, switching jobj.c from NonMatching to Matching in configure.py, and replacing the obj_files.mk entry so the build uses src/sysdolphin/baselib/jobj.c.o. The only C-side logic change shown is a cleanup in HSD_JObjUnrefThis: a local alternate iref_DEC helper was removed after object.h's ref_DEC and iref_DEC inline forms were reshaped to produce the needed codegen.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1364/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1360: Use `objdiff-cli report` to check for regressions

Status: agent_completed
Type: ci-regression-checking
Systems: .github;GitHub Actions;objdiff-cli;build workflow

Updated the GitHub Actions build workflow so regression checking uses `objdiff-cli report` output instead of comparing aggregate `progress.json` code/data percentages. The new diff-mode path generates actual and expected objdiff reports, computes a changes report, and fails CI when any function regresses from 100% fuzzy match to a lower match percentage.

## PR #1348: Check for progress regressions in CI

Status: agent_completed
Type: ci-workflow
Systems: .github;CI;build workflow;progress tracking

The build matrix was simplified from ad-hoc options to explicit modes, with link mode building the PR, saving its progress.json, switching to the repository default branch, rebuilding, and comparing DOL code/data progress with jq. CI now emits GitHub error annotations and fails if either code or data progress decreases. The workflow also adjusted upload conditions to use the new matrix.mode and added sparse checkout settings to a later checkout step.

## PR #1311: Match `ftCo_800A1994` (144 bytes)

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;ftCommon;asm

Matched the 144-byte fighter common function `ftCo_800A1994` in `src/melee/ft/chara/ftCommon/ftCo_0A01.c`, replacing the inline asm placeholder with C that normalizes signed byte field `x1A8E` to a float in [-1.0, 1.0]. The function now reads `fp->x1A8E` as `s8`, divides positive values by `127.0f` and non-positive values by `128.0f`, then clamps above `+1.0f` and below `-1.0f`. The PR report shows `ftCo_800A1994` at 100.00% with score 0.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1311/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1297: Implement "code bytes to go for next trophy"

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_progress_output
Systems: tools/project.py;progress.json output;build tooling

Added a progress-reporting line to tools/project.py that tells contributors how many code bytes remain until the next fancy progress item/trophy. The calculation reuses the already-displayed floored code trophy count, derives bytes per fancy fraction from code_total / code_fancy_frac, rounds the next threshold up with math.ceil, and subtracts current code_progress. The PR also includes small Python formatting/import cleanup in the same file.

## PR #1229: Fix `STATIC_ASSERT` condition

Status: agent_completed
Type: fix
Systems: src;Runtime;platform

Changed the preprocessor condition guarding the custom `STATIC_ASSERT` macro in `src/Runtime/platform.h` from `#ifdef _Static_assert` to `#ifdef __MWERKS__`. This makes the fallback struct-based assertion path explicitly depend on the Metrowerks compiler rather than testing for `_Static_assert`, avoiding the wrong branch selection when `_Static_assert` has been aliased or defined in the surrounding compatibility code.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1229/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling-update
Systems: tools/revert_asm.py;asm;config/GALE01;lbDvd

Updated the asm reversion workflow so `tools/revert_asm.py` no longer directly invokes `dtk elf disasm` on an object file. Instead it builds `ninja all_source` and copies the generated split asm from `build/GALE01/asm/...` back into `asm/...`, aligning reverted asm with the project's `dtk dol split` output format. Type: decomp-matching fix
Systems: sysdolphin;baselib;lobj;build configuration

Restored sysdolphin/baselib/lobj.c to Matching by reverting a prior nonmatching workaround around HSD_LightID2Index. The function return type and local index were changed from s32 to u32, the header prototype was updated to match, and the invalid GXLightID case now uses HSD_ASSERT(1170, 0) without assigning a fallback index. ## PR #1165: Match `mpColl_800477E0`

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: melee-core;mp;mpcoll;asm;headers;repo-root

Matched the map-collision function `mpColl_800477E0` in `src/melee/mp/mpcoll.c`, resolving #1064. The implementation preserves/restores ECB bottom coordinates when `x130_flags & 0x10` is set, updates collision state through existing `mpColl_*` helpers, computes a static boolean `mpColl_804D649C` from ECB correction extents against `flt_804D7FD8`, calls `mpColl_80043754(mpColl_80046904, arg0, 1U)`, forwards the result into `mpColl_80043324`, and returns it as `bool`. The PR also reorganized `mpcoll.h` into an address-commented prototype list, moved the old broader header contents into a new `mpcoll.dox`, and updated object-file bookkeeping.

## PR #1146: Use `Unknown` in `configure.py` until status is confirmed

Status: agent_completed
Type: build_configuration_status_semantics
Systems: repo-root;build configuration;object library lists;melee libraries;runtime libraries;dolphin libraries;sysdolphin

PR 1146 made a semantic-only status cleanup in `configure.py`: it introduced an `Unknown = False` status for objects whose matching status had not yet been confirmed, documented it as "Unconfirmed, might link as-is or with minor changes", and reserved `NonMatching = False` for files that are "Definitely not matching yet". Hundreds of object entries across Melee, runtime, Dolphin, and sysdolphin library lists were changed from `Object(NonMatching, ...)` to `Object(Unknown, ...)` without changing build behavior, because both constants evaluate to `False`.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1146/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1120: Fix include paths in `sysdolphin`

Status: agent_completed
Type: build_include_path_fix
Systems: src/sysdolphin/baselib;sysdolphin;baselib

Mass include-path cleanup across src/sysdolphin/baselib to work around an MWCC `-cwd source` header-resolution bug. The PR replaced many angle-bracket baselib includes such as `#include <baselib/jobj.h>` with quoted includes that are either local to the same baselib directory, such as `#include "jobj.h"`, or explicitly rooted as `#include "baselib/jobj.h"` inside headers. This mattered because MWCC resolves quoted includes from the current C file location rather than from the including header's directory when `-cwd source` is used, so insufficiently specific header paths could make unrelated translation units search the wrong directory, e.g. `fighter.c` including `gobj.h` then trying to resolve `melee/ft/jobj.h` instead of the baselib header.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1120/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed
Type: build_dependency_fix
Systems: configure.py;sysdolphin/baselib;dolphin/os;placeholder;melee/ftCommon

## PR #1055: Cleanup OS layout based on other projects, match some functions

Status: agent_completed
Type: cleanup-plus-decomp-matching
Systems: dolphin-os;dolphin-card;dolphin-exi;dolphin-si;dolphin-pad;dolphin-dvd;fighter;stage;build-system;.vscode

Large Dolphin OS cleanup aligning header/file layout with other decomp projects while also converting several previously handwritten or stubby OS routines into C or cleaner inline assembly. The PR renamed the OS umbrella header from src/dolphin/os/os.h to src/dolphin/os.h, removed src/dolphin/os/forward.h, updated many includes, normalized Pad file casing to lowercase pad.c/pad.h/pad.s, moved/expanded OS typedefs and structs into concrete subsystem headers, and matched/cleaned functions in OSContext, OSAlarm, OSInterrupt, OSMutex, OSThread, OSTime, OSSerial/SI, CARD/EXI, and related headers.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1055/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1052: Finish __strtoul match

Status: agent_completed
Type: decompilation-match
Systems: MSL;strtoul;stdio;wchar_io;build

Completed the C match for MSL __strtoul, allowing asm/MSL/strtoul.s to be deleted and removed from obj_files.mk. The replacement implements the base/max-width scanner as a state machine with ReadProc/fetch/unfetch callbacks, handling whitespace, signs, base autodetection including 0x, digit conversion, chars_scanned, negative, and overflow. The PR also cleaned related MSL stdio/wchar headers by removing duplicate FILE and enum definitions and updating fwide to use shared file_mode/orientation names. No PR body or review comments were present in the slice.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1052/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1051: Partial match strtoul

Status: agent_completed
Type: partial_decomp_match
Systems: MSL;MSL/strtoul;MSL/ctype;MSL/errno;MSL/printf;asm;obj_files.mk

Partially decompiled MSL strtoul support by adding a matching C implementation of the public strtoul wrapper while leaving the hard-to-match internal __strtoul body in assembly. The PR also promoted the ctype classification table and case maps into C as __ctype_map, __lower_map, and __upper_map, replaced assembly references to the old anonymous MSL_AnsiFp_803B8C30 table with __ctype_map, added MSL libc headers needed by the wrapper, and added errno.c/strtoul.c to obj_files.mk. The author explicitly noted that __strtoul differs from other library versions and appears to have a bug involving an unused spaces variable, so the scope was intentionally limited to what was needed to get strtoul matching.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1051/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1049: Match the initial GObj callback functions in some of the vi files

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: melee/vi;sysdolphin/baselib;obj_files.mk

Matched the first small callback functions in several melee/vi translation units by replacing leading assembly stubs with simple C wrappers. Most matched functions take an HSD_GObj*, load gobj->hsd_obj, cast it to HSD_JObj*, and call HSD_JObjAnimAll. One vi0601 callback simply forwards to un_8031CAAC. The PR added new vi source/header files and inserted their C object files before the remaining asm objects in obj_files.mk so only the newly matched functions moved out of assembly.

## PR #1042: Abolish `any_t`

Status: agent_completed
Type: type_cleanup
Systems: MSL;Runtime;dolphin/axfx;dolphin/os;melee/item;sysdolphin/baselib

Removed the project-wide `any_t` typedef from `src/Runtime/platform.h` and replaced its uses with plain `void*` across Dolphin OS/AXFX, sysdolphin baselib, and item code. The change made generic pointer APIs explicit without altering behavior: allocation hooks, heap APIs, stack pointer access, user-data callbacks, object update callbacks, and memory-forget callbacks now use `void*` directly. `NULL` in `src/MSL/stddef.h` was also changed from `((any_t) 0)` to `((void*) 0)`.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1042/postmortem/postmortem.json`

The PR also renamed several formerly address-like HSD_Video symbols to descriptive HSD_VI APIs, introduced real HSD_VIInfo/HSD_VIStatus/XFB/EFB state types, corrected Dolphin VI/GX prototypes, and adjusted CObj camera code to use HSD_VIGetRenderMode rather than treating HSD_VIData as a raw GXRenderModeObj. PR body explicitly notes that fixing the fakematch of GXSetDispCopySrc exposed an invalid cast, which was addressed by giving GXSetDispCopySrc/GXSetDispCopyDst and related GX framebuffer functions more accurate signatures.

## PR #1017: Create `SDATA` macro to force section

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: src;asm;fighter;ftCommon;ftafterimage;ftcolanim;dolphin-os;build-system

Added a portable `SDATA` macro in `src/placeholder.h` so C declarations can force placement into the `.sdata` section under Metrowerks, enabling small string literals previously kept in assembly stubs to move into C. The PR migrated `.sdata` strings for `ftCo_09CB`, `ftafterimage`, and `ftcolanim` from deleted asm files into their corresponding C files, removed those asm objects from `obj_files.mk`, and adjusted an `OSThread_data.s` `.skip` size. The stated motivation was that this allows small strings to be migrated to C and should make it possible to migrate anything from asm to C.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-1017/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #1016: Migrate `ftafterimage` to C

Status: agent_completed
Type: asm_to_c_migration
Systems: fighter;afterimage;item;GX;repo-root

Migrated the fighter afterimage unit from a large raw asm file into a new C translation unit while preserving matching assembly for `ftCo_800C2600` and `ftCo_800C2FD8`. The PR added `src/melee/ft/ftafterimage.c`, updated prototypes and inferred stack/header data, exposed jump tables and labels needed by inline asm, expanded fighter and sword item type layouts around afterimage-related offsets, and added the new C object to `obj_files.mk`. Evidence indicates this was primarily a relocation/matching-enabling migration rather than a full high-level C rewrite: the new C file uses `ASM` functions with `nofralloc` under `MUST_MATCH` and `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` otherwise.

Type: ci-docker-infrastructure

## PR #977: Match AXDriverUnlink, add HSD_SM struct

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: sysdolphin/baselib;melee/lb;asm;src

Matched AXDriverUnlink in C by introducing an HSD_SM sound-manager/list-node struct and moving the previous assembly implementation out of asm/sysdolphin/baselib/AXDriver.s. The PR also renamed two previously-address-only AXDriver symbols to HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffAll and HSD_AudioSFXKeyOffTrack and propagated those names through lbaudio_ax.s callsites. The HSD_SM layout was inferred loosely from a K7 version, with the author noting a deliberate difference around the 0x18 field: samID was omitted because the Melee code appears to use that byte as track, supported by an lbz access and surrounding K7-like function ordering.

## PR #956: frank.py: Restrict rescheduling to .text section

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_bugfix
Systems: tools;frank.py;object_file_processing

Updated tools/frank.py so its profiler-based instruction rescheduling operates only on the MWCC .text/code section rather than scanning and rewriting the entire object file. The script now extracts header, .text, and footer using the object code-size marker and a fixed MWCC code offset, runs rescheduling only on sliced code bytes, then reassembles the object. The PR also cleaned up repeated aligned-search logic and tightened safety checks around code-size mismatches and epilogue-local reorder handling.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-956/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #954: Remove itHit struct

Status: agent_completed
Type: types-structs
Systems: item;collision;library

Removed the item-local itHit struct and replaced item hitbox uses with the shared HitCapsule layout. The PR rationale was that item collision code passed &Item->x5D4 into functions expecting HitCapsule, and known regions of itHit and HitCapsule were identical. Item::x5D4_hitboxes became an array of small wrappers containing HitCapsule hit plus the trailing x138 field, function prototypes were updated from itHit* to HitCapsule*, and field accesses were translated from copied itHit names to HitCapsule names such as hit.state and hit.damage.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-954/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: repo-root;Makefile;tools;objdiff

Added an objdiff-focused make wrapper and a Makefile WIP mode. The new `tools/objdiff-make.sh` runs `make` with map generation, one-error limit, parallel jobs, `WINE=wibo`, and `WIP=1`; the Makefile uses `WIP=1` to skip checks, disable required prototypes, isolate outputs under `build/wip/ssbm.us.1.2`, and define `-DWIP` during compilation. This made objdiff iteration more convenient without disturbing the normal build directory.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-825/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #823: Clean up `Fighter` offset comments

Status: agent_completed
Type: header_cleanup
Systems: fighter;Donkey Kong fighter;Master Hand fighter;Sandbag fighter;ftcommon;ftdata;ftlib;ftdrawcommon;ftcliffcommon

Large mechanical cleanup of Fighter-related struct offset annotations and a small bitfield-access simplification. The main change was in src/melee/ft/types.h, where offsets in ftCommonData and Fighter were reformatted from generic 0x comments to clearer relative forms such as `/* +8 */`, `/* fp+2228 */`, and bit positions like `/* fp+2228:3 */`. The PR also flattened `x2228_flag` access from `x2228_flag.bits.bN` to `x2228_flag.bN` because the wrapper `bits` struct was removed, and reordered several fighter includes, apparently alphabetically. There were no PR body comments, review comments, or explicit rationale beyond the title, so the intent is inferred from the diff.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-823/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed
Type: naming_cleanup
Systems: fighter;fighter_parts;item;effects

Added Rust/Cargo tooling for synchronizing symbol names between linker maps and used it to import a large batch of symbol renames from PR #766. The PR body documents `melee-diff-symbols`, which compares two `GALE01.map` files and emits `FIND:REPLACE` pairs, and `melee-replace-symbols`, which applies those pairs across the working directory, including stdin piping with `-`. It also introduced/aided large-scale address-stable renames such as `func_8000AEC8` to `lbTime_8000AEC8`, many `func_`/`lbl_` symbols to `lb_8000....`, and casing cleanup like `lbvector_Normalize` to `lbVector_Normalize`. ## PR #760: Use `.textContent` to get text from the `#ctx-text` element

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_fix
Systems: tools;ctx_template;getting_started_context

Fixed the generated context-page copy button by reading `#ctx-text` with DOM `.textContent` instead of `.innerText`. The bug appeared when the target assembly text was collapsed: on Chrome/Windows, `innerText` could return an empty string because it reflects rendered/visible selectable text, while `textContent` returns the underlying node content regardless of collapsed display state. This made the docs/getting-started workflow more reliable for copying target assembly context.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-760/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #759: Create flags parser CLI tool

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: repo-root;tooling;tools/flags;cargo

Added a new Rust workspace tool, `melee-flags`, that parses C-style integer literals and emits copyable C declarations expressing the set bits as `1 << n` flag expressions. The tool supports hex, binary, octal, and decimal inputs, an optional `--signed` mode, automatic 32-bit versus 64-bit type selection, and comment-form warnings for invalid inputs.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-759/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #757: Fix cron jobs not pushing packages

Status: agent_completed
Type: ci_workflow_fix
Systems: .github;CI;GitHub Actions;package publishing

Fixed the package publishing workflow so scheduled cron runs can push packages. The workflow previously gated push steps on `github.event_name == 'push'`, which excluded scheduled builds; the PR changed those conditions to `github.event_name != 'pull_request'`, allowing both push and schedule-triggered jobs to publish while still preventing PR builds from pushing packages.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-757/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #756: Rewrite and improve `issues.sh`

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_rewrite
Systems: tools/issues;cargo-workspace;repo-root;compile_flags;clang-diagnostics

Replaced the old Bash `tools/issues.sh` clang diagnostic scraper with a Rust Cargo workspace tool at `tools/issues`. The new tool uses libclang through `clang-sys`, reads `compile_flags.txt`, scans `src/**/*.[ch]`, collects diagnostics from translation units, deduplicates them with a `HashSet`, sorts them deterministically, and prints grouped output by severity, diagnostic category, warning option, file, and source location. The PR also added Cargo/rustfmt configuration, a lockfile, ignores for Rust/Windows build artifacts, and an AGPL license for the new tool.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-756/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #755: Update `split_suggester.py` to widen use cases

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: tools;repo-root;split_suggester;requirements

Expanded and refactored `tools/split_suggester.py` so it could handle more assembly/data layouts and already-converted data syntax. The tool gained argparse-based CLI flags, a Python 3 shebang, explicit exit-code documentation, a `ProgramError` path, an `if __name__ == "__main__"`-style review-driven structure, support for `.float` and `.double` in `.sdata2` in addition to `.4byte`, preliminary bytecounting for additional sections, console-only/debug modes, map-file parsing as a function, and richer output including literals used per generated C file. `requirements.txt` added `black` and `isort`, likely to support formatting cleanup. The PR mattered because `split_suggester.py` became useful on files after `convert_data.py` had already run, letting contributors verify split suggestions against converted literals rather than only raw `.4byte` data.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-755/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed

Systems: tools/m2ctx;build context generation;MSL headers;stdarg header;requirements

Merged the old shell-based context generation workflow into tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py. The Python tool now discovers all src headers, writes build/ctx.h, preprocesses it with either mwcc or pcpp, optionally formats, colorizes, copies to clipboard, writes build/ctx.c, and supports quiet/no-file flags. The PR also adjusted headers so m2ctx can preprocess them more cleanly: removed src/MSL.h, renamed MSL math bit-access macros from __HI/__LO to MSL_HI/MSL_LO, and made stdarg.h handle M2CTX/non-MWERKS cases with placeholder types.

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: tools;git workflow

Added `tools/git_catchup.py`, a small GitPython-based helper script for catching a branch up to a target branch by checking out only files from the target that were not changed on the base branch. The PR body describes the intended workflow as taking a base branch, such as a PR branch, and a target branch, such as `master`, then applying target-side file updates except where the base branch already changed those files.

## PR #721: Improve m2c compatibility

Status: agent_completed
Type: m2c-compatibility-headers
Systems: m2c;runtime;platform;fighter;math;string

Added compatibility scaffolding for m2c valid-syntax output and adjusted unknown function prototypes so headers can serve both normal project compilation and m2c context generation. The PR introduced src/m2c_macros.h with M2C_UNK types and placeholder macros for m2c-emitted constructs, added UNK_T/UNK_RET/UNK_PARAMS abstractions in placeholder.h, switched a group of fighter header prototypes from concrete void(void) placeholders to unknown return/parameter macros, and made small platform/header changes for ATTRIBUTE_RESTRICT, ARRAY_SIZE, memcpy/m2c support, and __frsqrte declaration placement. The stated goal was to avoid the old unk_t/any_t split and move toward UNK_T plus void*, while still allowing m2c to infer types effectively under M2CTX.

Type: build-strictness-and-header-cleanup
Systems: repo-root;MSL;Runtime;MetroTRK;Dolphin;GX;CARD;DVD;sysdolphin-baselib;fighter;item;ground;melee-core

Enabled prototype-required builds and warning-as-error builds by default, forcing a broad cleanup of missing, malformed, or locally duplicated function declarations across MSL, Runtime, Dolphin, sysdolphin/baselib, fighter, item, ground, and related headers. The PR primarily converted source files to include their own public headers, added forward declarations and missing prototypes, corrected function signatures, removed redundant fallback stubs around MWERKS-only asm blocks, and moved struct forward declarations into forward headers where needed. The change mattered because it made missing prototypes and compiler warnings fail the build, raising the baseline for future decomp work and preventing silent signature drift.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-699/postmortem/postmortem.json`

It also adjusted a few function declarations/definitions to use explicit `void` parameter lists, exposed `__ARHandler` by removing `static`, added per-C-file headers for newly prototyped functions, and normalized some `struct _HSD_GObj*` prototypes to `HSD_GObj*` after including the proper gobj header.

This improves build/debug ergonomics without touching decompiled source.

## PR #690: Fix `clang` errors

Status: agent_completed
Type: build-portability-fix
Systems: Runtime;dolphin;dolphin-gx;dolphin-os;dolphin-base;dolphin-ai;fighter;item;stage;mp;sysdolphin-baselib

Large portability/build-cleanup PR that fixed all clang errors, not warnings, across Runtime, Dolphin OS/GX/base/AI, sysdolphin baselib, fighter, item, stage, and map collision code. The dominant tactic was to isolate CodeWarrior/MWCC-only inline assembly and matching-specific constructs behind `MWERKS_GEKKO`, `__MWERKS__`, or `MUST_MATCH`, then provide clang-parseable `NOT_IMPLEMENTED` fallbacks with corrected prototypes and headers. It also cleaned up several C type/prototype issues, flag macro naming, pointer-assignment expressions, and a baselib circular include via a small forward declaration header.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-690/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #688: Match dolphin ARQ

Status: agent_completed
Type: asm-to-c decompilation match
Systems: dolphin;dolphin/ar;ARQ;obj_files.mk

Replaced the hand-written assembly implementation of Dolphin ARQ with a matching C implementation and public header. The PR removed asm/dolphin/ar/arq.s, added src/dolphin/ar/arq.c and src/dolphin/ar/arq.h, and updated obj_files.mk to build arq.c.o instead of arq.s.o. The implementation models ARQ high/low request queues, pending requests, callbacks, chunked low-priority DMA transfers, DMA interrupt servicing, initialization, posting requests under disabled interrupts, and ARQGetChunkSize. The PR body says it was based on Prime decomp. There were no review comments; one collaborator review approved it.

## PR #673: Implement `stdbool.h`

Status: agent_completed
Type: header/API modernization
Systems: MSL;Runtime;Dolphin SDK;fighter;item;stage/ground;player;game-mode;sysdolphin baselib;melee-core

Added an MSL `stdbool.h` shim and migrated the codebase from project-local `BOOL`/`TRUE`/`FALSE` in `Runtime/platform.h` to standard-style `bool`/`true`/`false`. The change was broad but mostly mechanical, touching Dolphin libraries, Runtime, fighters, items, ground, player, baselib, and game-mode headers/sources. It centralized boolean definitions behind `<stdbool.h>` and removed the old `BOOL` typedef/macros from `platform.h`. Review discussion focused on whether the new header's documentation should claim it assumes GEKKO and on the fact that this shim uses `typedef int bool`, while the C standard's `_Bool` has unsigned-like behavior/representation concerns.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-673/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #672: Fix context syntax

Status: agent_completed
Type: context_syntax_header_fix
Systems: Runtime;MSL;MetroTRK;Dolphin OS init;Dolphin GX;Dolphin AI;fighter headers;m2ctx

Cherry-picked from #670, this PR cleaned up C/header syntax so the codebase remained usable for context generation while preserving MWERKS-specific behavior where needed. The main pattern was to hide compiler-only constructs such as __declspec(section ".init"), address-qualified declarations, and unknown-size arrays behind cross-tool macros in Runtime/platform.h, then apply those macros across startup/runtime/TRK headers. It also moved FILE/__file_modes structure definitions into stdio.h, trimmed duplicate or misplaced declarations from wchar_io and GX unknown headers, added small GX public headers/prototypes, and standardized fighter action enums to start character-specific IDs at ASID_MAX instead of redefining AS_FTCOMMON = 340 in each character header.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-672/postmortem/postmortem.json`

The PR also began a review-discussed naming cleanup for item callback fields, distinguishing boolean predicates from event callbacks, though the final convention was still being debated in review comments.

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_fix
Systems: tools/generate_context.sh;tools/m2ctx;MWCC preprocessing;context headers

Fixed the context-generation tooling by making `tools/generate_context.sh` produce a generated include aggregator under `build/`, run `m2ctx.py` with Python 3, optionally clang-format the generated context, and write `build/ctx.c`. `tools/m2ctx/m2ctx.py` was adjusted to resolve the repo root, use `src/MSL` instead of `include` as an include path, define `GEKKO`, and invoke the Windows MWCC preprocessor through `wine` on non-Windows hosts. Review discussion also captured temporary context-compilation fixes around `FILE` placement and enum sentinel naming, but those files were not present in the final changed-files list for this slice.

Type: headers-warning-cleanup
Systems: include;dolphin;sysdolphin-baselib;stage;ground;fighter;item;interface;player;game-mode;library

Large warning-cleanup/header pass aimed at eliminating implicit function declaration warnings while tightening type signatures across baselib, Dolphin math, fighter, stage, item, interface, and player code. The PR enabled stricter compile behavior by replacing `-Wno-unused-variable` with `-Werror`, added many missing prototypes, propagated more precise vector and allocation types, and performed a substantial `ground.c` cleanup. Review discussion captured useful decomp conventions around temporary marker types, padding arrays, macro style, and signed allocator size typedefs.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-658/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #657: Clean up `dolphin/types.h` and change empty brace formatting

Status: agent_completed
Type: cleanup
Systems: include;runtime;dolphin;fighter;stage;game-mode;sysdolphin-baselib

Moved the project’s common platform typedefs and constants out of `include/dolphin/types.h` into `include/Runtime/platform.h`, leaving `dolphin/types.h` as a thin compatibility/deprecated-macro wrapper. Added documentation comments for the platform types, introduced `enum_t` as an `int`-backed placeholder for not-yet-declared enums, introduced `any_t` as a `void*` placeholder distinct from `unk_t`, and adjusted several enum-like action-state locals/prototypes from `s32` to `enum_t`. The PR also changed `.clang-format` so empty functions may be formatted on one line, then applied that formatting broadly to empty stubs across dolphin, fighter, stage, game-mode, and baselib files.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-657/postmortem/postmortem.json`

The PR added explicit prototypes/includes, introduced a clearer MUST_MATCH build define for matching-only paths, moved platform/compiler macros into a new Runtime/platform.h, converted several NON_MATCHING conditionals to MUST_MATCH-first structure, and removed one Ness assembly file by moving/matching code into C. ## PR #627: Use AT_ADDRESS macro for fixed-address declarations

Status: agent_completed
Type: portability-refactor
Systems: include;dolphin;dolphin_os;dolphin_dsp;dolphin_gx;dolphin_card;dolphin_dvd

Introduced a portable AT_ADDRESS(x) macro in include/dolphin/types.h and converted fixed-address declarations across Dolphin OS, DSP, GX, CARD, DVD, and serial/time code from MWCC-only address syntax such as ': 0xCC005000' to declarations using AT_ADDRESS. The PR body states the purpose directly: the macro expands differently for PERMUTER, __MWERKS__, and other compilers so fixed-address declarations remain usable with decomp-permuter and non-MWCC compilers.

The PR body says this was "more copypasta from prime," and the diff supports a source-porting/matching strategy: CARDUnlock, CARDCheck, CARDMount, CARDFormat, CARDOpen, CARDRead/Write, CARDCreate/Delete/Stat/Rename, CARDBlock/CARDDir/CARDRdwr moved from asm objects to src objects in obj_files.mk. It also added or expanded support headers for CARD metadata, OSRtc SRAM structs, OSFont, OSReset constants, OSTime conversion/macros, and ATTRIBUTE_ALIGN. Review evidence is minimal: one approval with no review comments.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-618/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: dolphin/card;CARDBios;EXI;OSAlarm;OSReset;DVD;Makefile;obj_files.mk

Matched dolphin/card CARDBios by replacing the 1383-line assembly file with a 618-line C implementation, wiring it into obj_files.mk and the build's Frank post-processing path for src/dolphin/card objects. The PR also expanded dolphin/card.h with CARD result codes, CARDFileInfo/CARDDir/CARDControl layouts, callbacks, and internal prototypes, then updated remaining CARD asm files to reference the new C globals __CARDBlock and __CARDDiskNone instead of raw lbl_804A8B38-style labels. ## PR #614: Fix Fighter user_data accesses using `GET_FIGHTER` macro

Status: agent_completed
Type: fix
Systems: fighter;fighter-action-state;fighter-input;character-specials

Standardized many Fighter pointer fetches to use GET_FIGHTER instead of direct user_data access or getFighter/HSD_GObjGetUserData variants, and introduced union Struct2070 so the Fighter fields at 0x2070 can be accessed both as a packed s32 and as named bitfields. The main matching-relevant change replaced a volatile local stack workaround in Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC with a local union copy, preserving access to x2070_int while allowing x2071/x2072 bitfield members to live under fp->x2070. Review accepted the union as useful but left a todo to rename/investigate broader use.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-614/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #609: Improve mpcoll shiftability

Status: agent_completed
Type: asm shiftability cleanup
Systems: mpcoll;asm;melee-core;pl_unknown_001

Improved shiftability of the mpcoll assembly by replacing many hardcoded `.4byte` instruction encodings with normal PowerPC instructions that reference labels, and by adding `.set` aliases for mpcoll data and sdata2 labels relative to exported anchor symbols. The PR also adjusted mpcoll asserts in C to use a named global `lbl_804D3948` instead of the string literal `"0"`, matching asm references to that symbol. The author noted that offsets within the file were still hardcoded, so this was an incremental shiftability cleanup rather than full relocation independence.

The implementation preserves existing low-level DVD initialization behavior, including alarm setup, DVD filesystem and waiting-queue initialization, interrupt handler registration, thread queue initialization, direct DVD hardware register writes at 0xCC006000, boot magic checks, OSReport messages, and __fstLoad for JTAG boot.

## PR #581: Create a Getting Started guide for new contributors

Status: agent_completed
Type: documentation
Systems: docs;decomp.me workflow;contributor process

Added onboarding documentation for new Melee decomp contributors, centered on a new GETTING_STARTED.md explaining the project goal of matching PowerPC assembly 100%, basic decomp.me scratch workflow, context/header usage, how to choose unclaimed assembly, and starter resources. README.md was reorganized to link the new guide, add contributing/FAQ material, and discuss compiler/register-allocation uncertainty and permuter use. CONTRIBUTING.md gained auto-formatting guidance with git clang-format and clearer PR/onboarding advice, including the option to submit a decomp.me issue instead of a full PR. ## PR #580: Implement dump (`-d`) flag in `build.sh`

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: repo-root;build.sh;dump-generation;expected-artifacts

Added a `-d`/dump mode to `build.sh` that, after building, regenerates map data with `tools/parse_map.py` and runs `dadosod` on `build/ssbm.us.1.2/main.dol` to populate a local `dump/` directory. When combined with `-e`, the generated dump is also synchronized into `expected/dump`. The PR also added `dump/` to `.gitignore` so generated disassembly output is not committed.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-580/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #579: Migrate `os` to C

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: dolphin/os;asm;src;include;build-system

Large experimental migration of Dolphin `os` translation units from standalone `.s` assembly into C files, often using C with inline assembly plus separate `_data.s` files for remaining data sections. The stated goal was to make even very large TUs easier to work on incrementally: move `.text` into C first, create headers and provisional types, identify data, later migrate data and match functions. The PR removed full assembly sources such as `asm/dolphin/os/OSThread.s`, `OSReset.s`, `OSResetSW.s`, and `OSMemory.s`, added or expanded corresponding `src/dolphin/os/*.c` files, split data into files like `OSCache_data.s`, `OSContext_data.s`, `OSInterrupt_data.s`, and updated headers/build lists. Review discussion noted that much SDK OS code is handwritten assembly and therefore may be low-value or busy work for new contributors, while the author argued that placeholder `unk_t` signatures are useful because compiler errors force real signatures when symbols are used elsewhere.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-579/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #574: func_8007B5AC match

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: fighter;ftcoll;hurtbox/collision

Attempted to add a C match for fighter collision function func_8007B5AC in ftcoll.c plus a header prototype in ftcoll.h, but the PR was closed unmerged and explicitly marked as superseded by #576. The proposed implementation initialized ftHurt fields from an s32 data array, resolved a hurt bone JObj via Fighter.x5E8_fighterBones and x40_bone_index, byte-copied Vec3-like float data through s32 pointers to avoid floating-point register/double behavior, set x1C_scale, and set Fighter.x221A_flag.bits.b6. Review focused less on the local C body and more on decomp build/linking mechanics: removing a function from the middle of ftcoll.s breaks object ordering and matching unless the whole asm unit is handled correctly or both C and asm are wired through obj_files.mk.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-574/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #569: Match __OSInitSystemCall

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: src;dolphin;os;OSSync

Matched __OSInitSystemCall in src/dolphin/os/OSSync.c by replacing the NON_MATCHING C/handwritten asm fallback with a compact C implementation. The matching version stores OS_SYS_CALL_HANDLER in a local handler variable, copies the system call vector by subtracting the start/end function addresses as u32 values, flushes data cache, executes __sync, and invalidates instruction cache. The PR also removed unnecessary #pragma push/pop around the nearby asm vector wrapper.

## PR #531: Use new `calcprogress`

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_update
Systems: tools/calcprogress;Makefile;.github/workflows

Replaced the old single-file `tools/calcprogress.py` with a vendored/modular `tools/calcprogress/` implementation and updated the Makefile and GitHub Actions workflow to call the new path and CLI. The new tool calculates decomp progress by reading DOL sizes, parsing CodeWarrior map section header symbols, reading assembly files listed by `obj_files.mk`, summing remaining assembly section sizes, and reporting source bytes as total minus assembly bytes. It preserves Melee-specific trophy/event progress output through a callback.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-531/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: sysdolphin/baselib/controller;sysdolphin/baselib/rumble;melee/gm/gmmain;melee/lb/lbunknown_005

Renamed several previously anonymous HSD controller/pad functions in baselib assembly and updated C/assembly callsites to use the new HSD_Pad* labels. The PR primarily improved symbol clarity around controller initialization, status renewal, clamp checks, AD conversion checks, cross-direction handling, and rumble initialization without changing behavior.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-508/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #507: Match `DSPAddTask`

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: dolphin;dsp;asm;src

Matched the Dolphin DSP library function `DSPAddTask` in C and removed its handwritten assembly implementation from `asm/dolphin/dsp/dsp.s`. The new C implementation disables interrupts, inserts the task into the DSP task queue, initializes `task->state` and `task->flags`, restores interrupts, boots the task if it became `__DSP_first_task`, and returns the input task pointer. The PR was tracked in issue #480 and had no review comments in the provided slice.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-507/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #506: Match `dvdqueue`

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: dolphin;dvd;osinterrupt;build-system

Matched the Dolphin DVD waiting queue module by replacing asm/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.s with src/dolphin/dvd/dvdqueue.c and switching obj_files.mk to build the C object. The new C source implements the queue sentinel array, interrupt-protected push/pop/check/dequeue helpers, and preserves the assembly behavior closely enough to remove the 160-line assembly file. The PR body only says it was tracked in #480, and there were no review comments.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-506/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed

Systems: tools;asm;src/sysdolphin/baselib;include/dolphin/os;include/dolphin/pad;src/dolphin/os

The PR also carried a demonstration/application against `sysdolphin/baselib/baselib_unknown_002`, adding many provisional prototypes, Dolphin OS reset/PAD headers, reset-switch structs, and decomp.me scratch links while removing unnecessary `.global` directives from local labels. The PR was closed because it was superseded by #454.

## PR #414: Use shasum command on macOS, sha1sum otherwise

Status: agent_completed
Type: build-system-portability
Systems: repo-root;Makefile;toolchain-setup;checksum-verification

Updated the root Makefile so the SHA1 checksum command is selected by host OS: macOS/Darwin uses `shasum`, while other platforms use `sha1sum`. This moved SHA1SUM setup out of the Windows/non-Windows split, making checksum verification work on macOS without breaking Linux or Windows-style builds.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-414/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #404: Match HSD gobjobject

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: sysdolphin;baselib;gobj;objalloc;include;obj_files.mk
