The new C covers lookup by classifier within the r13 entity pointer lists, attaching object data to an HSD_GObj with an assert, detaching object data, and destroying attached object data via a kind-indexed callback table. Supporting header changes clarified HSD_GObj::obj_kind as unsigned, introduced HSD_GOBJ_OBJ_NONE as 0xFF, gave func_80390A70 a typed prototype, and changed struct r13_EntityPointers from filler bytes to an HSD_GObj* x0[8] array.

## PR #401: Change github actions from wine to wibo

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: .github;GitHub Actions;WiBo;Metrowerks compiler tooling;map/progress tooling

Reworked the GitHub Actions build workflow to use WiBo instead of Wine for running the Metrowerks tools in CI, motivated by spurious Wine failures. The workflow now installs multilib/i386 dependencies rather than wine32, checks out and builds decompals/WiBo, invokes make with WINE pointing at the built wibo executable, verifies and patches mwldeppc.exe with SHA1 checks before and after, uploads GALE01.map for map-generating builds, and appends match-progress output to the GitHub step summary.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-401/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Introduced Captain Falcon fighter decomp scaffolding by moving/splitting the old Falcon assembly into ftCaptain-specific asm files, adding a new ftCaptain header with CaptainAttr special-attribute layout, adding a small C file for Captain Falcon lifecycle/item/attribute callbacks, and updating obj_files.mk so the new C object and asm fragments replace the former monolithic ftfalcon.s object. The PR body says the work was done by Eric on Discord and adjusted by BR- to be acceptable for PR submission.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-380/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #379: add new Makefile setting to limit number of errors printed per file

Status: agent_completed
Type: build-system-quality-of-life
Systems: repo-root;Makefile;CodeWarrior CFLAGS

Added a root Makefile knob, MAX_ERRORS, that passes CodeWarrior's -maxerrors option through CFLAGS. This lets developers run commands such as `make MAX_ERRORS=1` to limit compiler diagnostics per file and avoid console flooding during decompilation work.

## PR #376: Remove negative sized padding

Status: agent_completed
Type: struct_layout_fix
Systems: fighter

Removed an invalid/negative padding region in the Fighter struct around x1064_thrownHitbox. The PR explains that sizeof(ftHit) is 0x138, making the old filler expression `0x1198 - 0x1064 - sizeof(ftHit)` evaluate to -4. The fix treats the field previously labeled `x1198` as overlapping with `x1064_thrownHitbox.x134`, updates Fighter_ActionStateChange_800693AC to write that ftHit subfield directly, and deletes the bogus filler and `x1198` struct member.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-376/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #349: ci: Add GitHub action

Status: agent_completed
Type: ci_tooling
Systems: .github/workflows;Makefile;tools/calcprogress.py;devkitPPC;Wine;MWCC compiler setup

Added a GitHub Actions build workflow that builds the repository on push and pull_request using a devkitPPC container, downloads the required MWCC compiler bundle, runs make with GENERATE_MAP=1, and therefore exercises calcprogress in CI. The Makefile was adjusted for Unix CI defaults by silencing Wine debug output, setting a default DEVKITPPC path, and passing explicit DOL and MAP paths to calcprogress.py. calcprogress.py was made more reusable by reading the DOL and map file paths from command-line arguments instead of hardcoded project paths.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-349/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #348: Generate vanilla and profile .o files separately

Status: agent_completed
Type: build-system-refactor
Systems: repo-root;Makefile;epilogue-processing;frank;mwcc;melee;sysdolphin

Reworked the build system so normal MWCC output and frank/profile-patched output are compiled as separate object trees under build/$(TARGET)/vanilla and build/$(TARGET)/profile, then merged into the final build objects. This replaced the old epilogue/ directory and deleted the manually maintained e_files.mk list of epilogue-unscheduled objects. The stated motivation was easier inspection of vanilla versus profile objects and faster builds because the two object variants can be generated independently and in parallel.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-348/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Systems: fighter;ftdrmario;melee/ft/chara;build-system;headers

Matched Dr. Mario fighter code by deleting the remaining asm file for ft/chara/ftdrmario and replacing it with C across ftdrmario.c plus a small separate translation unit, ftdrmario_2.c. The separate TU was intentional: the PR author stated func_80149954 had to be split out to get correct relocations for a function pointer it uses. The change also expanded ftdrmario.h prototypes and ftDrMarioAttributes, added Fighter struct fields needed by this code, and updated build lists so ftdrmario.o and ftdrmario_2.o are built from src rather than asm. Reviewer feedback highlighted that a placeholder '_2' filename should ideally be replaced by a Special-specific name, probably SpecialN, because ft files should be split by Specials.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-295/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #294: Cleanup alignment through pl code and the sysdolphin section

Type: assembly_alignment_cleanup

Large assembly-only cleanup to repair section alignment across player-related melee code and many sysdolphin/baselib assembly files. The main structural change split the large asm/melee/lb/code_80019230.s range at 0x80019880 into a new asm/melee/lb/code_80019880.s object, and obj_files.mk was updated accordingly. Many files gained explicit `.balign 8` immediately after `.section` declarations for rodata, data, bss, sdata, sbss, and sdata2, while some padding-like NULL words were removed where the corrected section alignment made them unnecessary. The PR body states that remaining troublesome alignment cases were intentionally split out for later.

The author framed it as a coordination/synchronization step: keep fighter.h in sync, expose all fighter functions in C form so contributors could decompile individual functions out of order, and avoid linking fighter.c until all functions matched. Closed, unmerged WIP attempt to decompile and name the large player module. The PR replaced much of asm/melee/pl/player.s with a very large src/melee/pl/player.c implementation gated by an explicit `#define NON_MATCHING 1`, expanded src/melee/pl/player.h with a more detailed StaticPlayer layout and many Player_* prototypes, and propagated new Player_* symbol names through many asm callsites. ## PR #202: Match HSD_MemAlloc

Type: decomp-matching
Systems: sysdolphin;baselib;memory;shadow;dobj;include;asm;repo-root

Matched HSD_MemAlloc in C and removed its assembly object from the build. ## PR #185: Add Cam's match of fmod

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: src;sysdolphin;baselib;aobj

Replaced an inline PowerPC assembly implementation of the local `fmod` helper in `src/sysdolphin/baselib/aobj.c` with a short matching C implementation attributed by the PR title to Cam. The function keeps the same observed behavior: if `fabs(b) > fabs(a)`, return `a`; otherwise compute an integer quotient from `a / b` and return `a - b * quotient`. This removed a block of hand-written assembly around `__cvt_dbl_usll` / `__cvt_sll_flt` calls and converted it into maintainable C while preserving the match.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-185/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Type: asm-data-section-split
Systems: Makefile;asm;MSL;MetroTRK;Runtime;Dolphin JPEG;sysdolphin baselib;melee fighter;melee item;melee stage;melee game/menu

Moved large monolithic small-data and rodata assembly blobs into the asm translation-unit files that actually own those constants or globals. The Makefile stopped linking a separate RODATA_O_FILES group, while asm/rodata.s, asm/sdata.s, and asm/sbss.s were largely emptied. Many MSL, MetroTRK, Dolphin, SysDolphin, fighter, item, menu, and stage asm files gained local .rodata, .sdata, or .sbss sections containing labels that had previously lived in global section files. This mattered because it made data ownership more local and enabled future per-file decompilation/matching without carrying a single centralized rodata object.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-174/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: src;sysdolphin;baselib

## PR #155: improve frank efficiency

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling-optimization
Systems: tools/frank.py;Makefile;epilogue-processing

PR #155 optimized and simplified the epilogue-fixup tooling path by making tools/frank.py handle work previously split with tools/postprocess.py, then updated the Makefile to remove POSTPROC/PROCFLAGS and run Frank directly in quiet epilogue-processing rules. The Frank changes replaced count-based branch restoration with aligned byte-sequence scanning for BLR-family instructions and added logic to reunify separated mtlr/blr instruction pairs by shifting intermediary instructions upward. The author summarized it as simplified, faster code that obsoletes postprocess.py and still matches.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-155/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #154: frank works better now

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling-fix
Systems: tools;makefile;repo-root;include;lb;sysdolphin-baselib

PR 154 improved the Epilogue-assisted build/postprocessing path centered on tools/frank.py. frank.py stopped detecting end-of-code via fragile terminal byte-sequence scans and instead derived the code section end from a code-size field found after a CODESIZE_MAGIC marker. The Makefile was updated to use Epilogue mwcceppc 1.2.5e, run frank before postproc for EPILOGUE_PROCESS objects, clean the epilogue directory, and apply the lbvector CFLAGS override to the Epilogue object too. e_files.mk added several additional Epilogue-unscheduled objects. Ignore rules were expanded for generated Windows binaries/batch files and include/*.s. The PR body states that the result matches and that postprocessor.py is only needed to fix stray mtlr instructions, but there was little review discussion.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-154/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #143: Fix write error and handle other mr cases

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_fix
Systems: tools/postprocess.py;ELF postprocessing;old stack handling

Fixed a small but important bug in tools/postprocess.py: after reading the instruction at mr_pos, the script now seeks back to mr_pos before writing, preventing the write from landing at the post-read file offset. The postprocess pattern matcher was also broadened to recognize several valid `mr r3, r28/r29/r30/r31` encodings after an `lwz r0, N(r1)` instruction, instead of only one hard-coded `mr` case.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-143/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #142: Fix copy paste mistake on postprocess.py

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_bugfix
Systems: tooling;ELF postprocessing

Fixed a one-line copy/paste bug in tools/postprocess.py inside impl_postprocess_elf. When collecting mr_epilogues, the script previously appended (lwz_pos, it), but the surrounding condition was checking both lwz_pos and mr_pos; the fix correctly appends (lwz_pos, mr_pos). This keeps the postprocess epilogue tuple consistent with the detected lwz/mr positions rather than using the current iterator value.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-142/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #133: Fix non-readable segment warnings

Status: agent_completed
Type: build/asm section metadata fix
Systems: MSL abort/exit assembly;Dolphin OS init assembly;constructors/destructors sections

Small assembly section-attribute fix: the PR changed the handwritten assembly declarations for the .dtors and .ctors sections to include writable/allocatable flags, replacing bare `.section .dtors` and `.section .ctors` with `.section .dtors, "wa"` and `.section .ctors, "wa"`. This addressed non-readable segment warnings while preserving the existing labels and data layout.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-133/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #132: Fix lbsnap split, move some of the bss into files, split sbss out of bss.s

Status: agent_completed
Type: asm_data_layout_split
Systems: asm;bss;sbss;lb;cm;obj_files.mk

PR 132 was a low-level assembly/data-layout cleanup. It fixed the lbsnap split by moving func_8001D2BC from asm/melee/lb/code_80016A54.s into asm/melee/lb/lbsnap.s and adjusted lbsnap's text range comment from 0x8001D338 to 0x8001D2BC. It also reduced the monolithic asm/bss.s by moving several early .bss labels into their owning asm files and extracted the entire .sbss block into a new asm/sbss.s file wired through obj_files.mk.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-132/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #131: Split lbcollision code out properly based on sdata usage

Status: agent_completed
Type: assembly_object_split
Systems: asm/melee/lb;lbcollision;sdata;sdata2;obj_files.mk

Reorganized lb assembly object boundaries around lbcollision by moving code and its small-data constants into the objects that actually use them. The old asm/melee/lb/code_80008D30.s file was removed, asm/melee/lb/code_80005BB0.s was renamed to asm/melee/lb/lbcollision.s and expanded with the 0x80008D30-era collision routines plus their .sdata/.sdata2 labels, a new asm/melee/lb/code_8000AD8C.s object was added for the following address range, and root asm/sdata.s and asm/sdata2.s lost the labels now owned by those lb objects. obj_files.mk was updated so the build uses lbcollision.o and code_8000AD8C.o instead of code_80005BB0.o and code_80008D30.o.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-131/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #130: Fix postprocess assert caused by properly ordered code

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling_fix
Systems: tools;postprocess.py

Fixed a tooling-side postprocess.py assertion triggered by code whose epilogue instructions appeared in a valid order. The change separates tracking of an `addi r1, r1, 0x18` stack-restore instruction from tracking of `mtlr`, so the postprocessor can record an epilogue ending in `blr` without misclassifying the `addi` as an `mtlr` position.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-130/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #129: Patch postprocess.py to make lbcommand match

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching-tooling
Systems: tools/postprocess.py;melee/lb;asm;sdata2

Adjusted the ELF postprocess epilogue-detection logic so the C implementations of lbcommand timer commands could match, allowing inline assembly fallbacks for Command_04 and Command_08 to be removed. The PR also moved lbl_804D79E8 from the global asm/sdata2.s block into asm/melee/lb/lbcommand.s while removing lbl_804D79E0 from asm/sdata2.s, reflecting the new matching output's data needs.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-129/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #86: Remove hardcoded _SDA(2)_BASE_ usage

Status: agent_completed
Type: assembly-syntax-cleanup
Systems: asm;MSL;Dolphin SDK;sysdolphin/baselib;melee;fighter;items;stages;menus;game;graphics;audio

Large mechanical assembly cleanup replacing hardcoded small-data base expressions like lbl-_SDA_BASE_(r13) and lbl-_SDA2_BASE_(r2) with the assembler relocation form lbl@sda21(r13/r2), plus bare addi forms using lbl@sda21. The PR touched 210 assembly files across MSL, Dolphin SDK, sysdolphin/baselib, and many Melee game systems with near-equal added/deleted line counts, indicating a broad syntax normalization rather than behavioral code changes.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-86/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #85: Switch to using the 1.3.2 linker

Status: agent_completed
Type: build-toolchain
Systems: Makefile;asm;sdata;extabindex;src/runtime;C++ exception runtime

Switched the build to use the Metrowerks 1.3.2 linker while keeping the compiler at MWCC 1.0. The stated motivation was link-time speed: 1.3.2 was faster than the previous linker and less difficult to adopt than 2.7. The change required a small Makefile split between compiler and linker versions plus minor symbol/layout adjustments around C++ exception initialization data, including naming lbl_804D5B40 as fragmentID.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-85/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #51: Elf2dol wii changes

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: repo-root;tooling;elf2dol;Makefile

Updated the repo build pipeline and tools/elf2dol.c so elf2dol receives a target platform string and can account for Wii-style duplicated PDHR size handling while preserving the default GameCube build path. The Makefile now defines TARGET_COL as gamecube and passes it to ELF2DOL. elf2dol now tracks sdata and sbss sizes in two-element arrays and, when platform is "wii", also captures the sizes at sdata_pdhr + 2 and sbss_pdhr + 2 before adding all tracked sizes into the DOL BSS size.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-51/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #35: remove verbosity flags from elf2dol call

Status: agent_completed
Type: build-system-cleanup
Systems: repo-root;build;elf2dol

Small build-system cleanup in the repository root Makefile: the DOL build rule stopped passing two verbose flags to elf2dol. The command changed from `$(ELF2DOL) -v -v $< $@ $(SDATA_PDHR) $(SBSS_PDHR)` to `$(ELF2DOL) $< $@ $(SDATA_PDHR) $(SBSS_PDHR)`, while preserving the same ELF input, DOL output, SDATA/SBSS program-header arguments, and SHA1 verification step.

## PR #34: Switch lcf to use template and refactor build system to use template.

Status: agent_completed
Type: build_tooling_refactor
Systems: repo-root;tooling;asm;src;linker

Refactored the early Melee build/link pipeline so object link order moved out of the linker script into a new obj_files.mk, while ldscript.lcf switched from a long hand-maintained section/object listing to a template-style LCF with grouped aligned sections. The PR also adjusted elf2dol to account for sdata/sbss program-header sizes when computing DOL BSS size, marked __mem functions into .init, and changed asm/bss.s sizing to remove the previous sdata/sbss placement hack.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-34/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #33: Preprocess lcf and start purging lcf pointers

Status: agent_completed
Type: build_linker_script_preprocessing
Systems: Makefile;ldscript.lcf;asm;include

Made the linker command file preprocessable and began replacing hard-coded LCF section addresses with derived segment symbols/macros. The Makefile now generates build/ldscript.lcf via cpp, uses that generated script for linking, adds a default/all target, enables gcc-style MWCC diagnostics, runs elf2dol more verbosely, and simplifies clean to remove the build directory. The linker script now defines BEGIN_SEG/END_SEG macros, chains most section starts from prior segment ends, adds temporary hacks for sdata/sdata2 positioning, and computes _SDA_BASE_/_SDA2_BASE_ from linker-derived positions instead of fixed literal addresses. A matching-support symbol _SDA_BASE_START was added in asm/sdata2.s, and newline-only cleanup occurred in include/global.h and asm/bss.s.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-33/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #27: setup postprocess.py

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling
Systems: repo-root;tooling;Makefile;CodeWarrior build

Added a Python ELF postprocessor and wired it into the C object build rule. The tool supports three CodeWarrior-oriented fixes: ctor/dtor section alignment repair, mangled-symbol character substitution reversal, and old-stack prologue/epilogue scheduling fixups. The Makefile now invokes python3 tools/postprocess.py after each C compilation with -fprologue-fixup=old_stack, and adds -nodefaults to CFLAGS.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-27/postmortem/postmortem.json`

Status: agent_completed
Type: decomp-matching
Systems: asm/init;src;Makefile;ldscript.lcf;repo-root

Replaced the hand-written assembly for the early .init memory routines with matching C for memset and __fill_mem, added the new source/header files, adjusted the linker script so __mem.o(.text) is placed at the start of .init at 0x80003100, and changed compiler flags from -O2,p to -O4,p because -O2,p did not match due to variable ordering in memset. The Makefile also gained an INCLUDES variable covering include and src, and ssbm.sha1 was normalized by removing a problematic trailing carriage return/no-newline issue.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-6/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #5: Split some assembly files

Status: agent_completed
Type: asm-reorganization
Systems: asm;linker-script;repo-root

Split a large monolithic assembly text file into several smaller asm files while preserving link order through ldscript.lcf. The PR moved roughly the early .text range starting at 0x80005940 out of asm/text.s into address-named files plus asm/lbvector.s, then explicitly listed those objects in the .text section before the remaining text.o.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-5/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #4: Add another possible shell uname for checking Windows

Status: agent_completed
Type: build_system_portability
Systems: repo-root;build

Updated the root Makefile's Windows environment detection to recognize MSYS in addition to MINGW. The PR body says the existing name check was not sufficient, while checking MSYS correctly caught the author's terminal and set the WINDOWS flag.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-4/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #3: Add numerous JObj symbols and HSD_EraseRect

Status: agent_completed
Type: symbolication
Systems: asm/disasm;HSD;JObj;screen_clearing

Large assembly symbolication pass in asm/disasm.s that replaced many anonymous func_ labels and call targets with known HSD JObj names, plus named func_80374AA0 as HSD_EraseRect. The PR body says the JObj symbols came from past work and that HSD_EraseRect is used for screen clearing. Evidence indicates this was a naming-only assembly update: one file changed, with equal added and deleted line counts, and the diff shows labels and branch-call target names changing while instruction addresses/opcodes remain the same.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-3/postmortem/postmortem.json`

## PR #2: set up disassembly

Status: agent_completed
Type: tooling/bootstrap
Systems: asm;repo-root;tooling;linker-script;dol-conversion

Initial repository setup for building a full-assembly Melee DOL from a generated disassembly. The PR added a very large asm/disasm.s, a root Makefile using devkitPPC assembler and Metrowerks linker/compiler under Wine, an ldscript.lcf fixing section virtual addresses and SDA bases, an elf2dol conversion tool, SHA1 verification for main.dol, and ignore rules for toolchain/build artifacts.

Postmortem JSON: `prs/pr-2/postmortem/postmortem.json`
